Title: Verticillium longisporum phospholipase VlsPLA2 is a virulence factor that targets host nuclei and modulates plant immunity

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds VlsPLA2 and is associated with its transport/localization to the plant nuclear membrane, facilitating host targeting linked to altered phospholipid-associated immunity modulation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural and signaling membrane lipids involved in plant immunity-related signal transduction
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: localized at the cell periphery when transiently expressed
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: transient agroinfiltration system; promoter not explicitly stated
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; interaction assayed at 48 hpi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction required intact NbVAMPA1 protein and its conserved DMQCKDKFL motif; MSP-only or MSP-deleted forms did not interact. Co-IP detected interaction with VlsPLA2ΔNLS1NLS2, while Y2H supported interaction with WT VlsPLA2 and NLS mutant; no interaction detected with catalytically inactive VlsPLA2ΔCD.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MS/MS pull-down candidate identification; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; confocal colocalization; truncation/motif-deletion interaction tests
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host susceptibility factor/trafficking component to target for reducing effector entry or nuclear membrane targeting, though direct breeding value not established
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbVAMPA1 was identified as a plant interactor of VlsPLA2. 2. It colocalized with VlsPLA2 at the nuclear membrane/cell periphery. 3. Its intact structure and conserved motif were required for interaction, implicating vesicle trafficking in effector delivery.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vesicle-associated membrane protein A; N-terminal MSP domain; C-terminal transmembrane domain

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Verticillium longisporum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Brassica napus
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Verticillium longisporum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Brassica napus
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Col-0 for transgenic plants; Brassica napus 'Hannah' for infection-expression analysis; Nicotiana benthamiana variety NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phospholipase A2 domain (IPR015141); signal peptide; two NLS motifs
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream membrane lipid hydrolysis step linked to signaling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: lysophospholipids/free fatty acid products; study specifically observed increased monoacyl-glycero-phosphocholine 16:0 and diacyl-glycero-phosphocholines 36:5 and 36:6 upon heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses phospholipids in membranes as a phospholipase A2; catalytic HD dyad is required for activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: modifies host phospholipid pool and likely generates signaling-active lipid products that alter immunity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active VlsPLA2 alters host phospholipid composition, suppresses chitin-induced ROS and Cf4/Avr4-triggered HR, and enhances Verticillium virulence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane structural components and signaling molecules relevant to basal immune responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plant cell membranes; nuclei/nuclear membrane; chloroplast-associated membranes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced during Brassica napus infection, with highest transcript accumulation at 6 dpi versus PDB-grown mycelia
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: When heterologously expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana, effects assayed at 48 and 72 hpi; in Arabidopsis transgenics expression increased susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA constitutive promoter in fungal overexpression strains; 35S promoter in Arabidopsis/Nicotiana expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host infection; transient leaf expression; chitin treatment for ROS assay; Cf4/Avr4 HR assay; Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 HR assay
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Initially host nucleus at 48 hpi, later chloroplasts and cell periphery at 72 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (V. longisporum and Nicotiana benthamiana), European Nucleotide Archive BioProject PRJEB55988; lipidomics by UHPLC-QTOF-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR infection expression analysis; purified enzyme assay; catalytic dyad mutagenesis; fungal overexpression virulence assays; Arabidopsis transgenic expression; lipidomics; ROS and HR suppression assays; confocal localization; RNA-seq; Y2H/co-IP interaction assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene deletion attempts failed, likely due to retention of WT locus in this hybrid fungus. Catalytically inactive VlsPLA2ΔCD lost enzymatic activity, could not suppress HR/ROS, did not interact with NbVAMPA1, and localized to chloroplasts rather than nucleus despite intact NLSs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhances pathogen virulence and host susceptibility; no agronomic benefit reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful anti-virulence target for crop protection strategies, including host-induced silencing or effector-targeted resistance engineering; catalytic dyad and host interaction surface are candidate intervention points
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VlsPLA2 is a secreted, active phospholipase A2 highly induced during host infection. 2. Its catalytic activity alters host phospholipid profiles and is required for suppression of ROS and PRR-triggered HR. 3. Overexpression increases fungal virulence and host susceptibility, while the protein initially targets host nuclei and interacts with NbVAMPA1.
